one_time_description: Gain on disposal of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: George Gordon Developments Limited Partnership and 4 Claw Retail Limited Partnership are government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. Gordon Retail Centre transferred operations to 4 Claw Retail Limited Partnership effective January 1, 2024. First Nation initiated a claim against Government of Canada for damages; obtained loan to fund negotiations with insurance coverage. TLE Holdings generates rental income. Dakota Dunes Community Development Corporation involvement noted.
notes: Province is Saskatchewan (SK), not AB. Consolidated entity includes 7 subsidiaries on line-by-line basis. Two GBEs (George Gordon Developments Limited Partnership and 4 Claw Retail Limited Partnership) accounted for under modified equity method. Gordon Retail Centre changed from consolidated to GBE treatment effective January 1, 2024 (change in reporting entity). TLE Trust Principal Account excluded from consolidation as Chief and Council do not control the Trust. Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding claim against Government of Canada. total_government_transfers includes ISC ($24,828,486) plus CMHC ($405,721 + $316,785 + $104,079 = $826,585) plus National Housing Co-Investment Fund ($70,150) minus ISC subtotal adjustments already in ISC line - calculated as ISC total $24,828,486 + CMHC $405,721 + CMHC rental $316,785 + CMHC RRAP $104,079 + NHCIF $70,150 = $25,725,221 approximately. expenses_education includes Education ($5,579,651) + Post Secondary ($1,757,874) = $7,337,525. Deferred revenue schedule was truncated in source text. Remuneration schedule not provided in extracted text.
